malay: Pistachio dipanggang kering, tanpa garam, dikupas
hindi: पிस்ता (सूखा भुना, बिना नमक, छिला हुआ)

region_of_origin: Central Asia and the Middle East (Pistacia vera native; major producers: Iran, USA, Turkey)
food_subgroup: Tree nuts — dry-roasted pistachio kernels, shelled, unsalted

ontology.foodex2_description: Pistachio nuts (Pistacia vera), kernel, dry roasted, without salt
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for dry-roasted pistachio kernels; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient for ontological coverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard clinical serving of dry-roasted unsalted pistachios is approximately 28g (about 49 kernels or one small handful), providing roughly 157 kcal, 5.7g protein, 12.7g fat, and 7.8g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Pistachios Dry Roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #12151
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12151 is an exact match for dry-roasted, unsalted, shelled pistachio kernels (Pistacia vera). All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for pistachio nuts
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~15 for pistachio nuts is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283. The very low GI reflects the high fibre (10.6g/100g) and fat (45.3g/100g) content that dramatically blunts postprandial glucose response. GL per 28g serving = (15 × 7.8g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 1.2, consistent with input value of 1.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (3mg/100g); very high potassium (1,025mg/100g); MUFA supports arterial elasticity
hypertension.guidance: Unsalted pistachios are highly suitable for hypertension management: sodium is negligible at 3mg/100g, potassium is among the highest of all tree nuts at 1,025mg/100g (287mg per 28g serving), and the high MUFA content (23.6g/100g) together with arginine-derived nitric oxide production supports vasodilation and improved arterial compliance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~15 (very low); GL ~1.1 per 28g serving; RCT evidence for fasting glucose reduction; second meal effect
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pistachios have a GI of approximately 15 and a glycaemic load of ~1.1 per 28g serving; multiple RCTs in T2DM patients (Kendall 2011, Parham 2014) demonstrate 5–10% reductions in fasting blood glucose at 25–85g/day, and the second meal effect — whereby pistachio consumption reduces glycaemic response at the subsequent meal by ~20–30% — makes them a clinically recommended snack for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High MUFA (23.6g/100g); high potassium; lutein + zeaxanthin; gamma-tocopherol anti-inflammatory properties; zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular pistachio consumption is associated with improved lipid profiles (reduced LDL, increased HDL) due to high MUFA (23.6g/100g) and PUFA (13.5g/100g) content; zero dietary cholesterol, high potassium (1,025mg/100g), and the unique gamma-tocopherol and lutein/zeaxanthin content (~1,200–1,600mcg/100g) provide additional cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory benefits, making pistachios a heart-healthy tree nut choice.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lutein + zeaxanthin anti-inflammatory; MUFA cardiovascular risk reduction; gamma-tocopherol
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pistachios provide lutein and zeaxanthin (~1,200–1,600mcg/100g) with demonstrated in-vitro anti-inflammatory activity (reduced TNF-α and IL-6), and gamma-tocopherol with unique anti-inflammatory properties; in RA patients where cardiovascular risk is doubled, the MUFA-rich profile (23.6g/100g) supports cardiovascular risk reduction alongside joint inflammation management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pistachios (Pistacia vera), dry roasted without salt, shelled kernels — per 100g: 562 kcal, 20.2g protein (highest protein content among common tree nuts), 45.3g fat (23.6g MUFA, 13.5g PUFA, 5.9g saturated fat), 27.7g carbohydrate, 10.6g dietary fibre (highest fibre among common tree nuts), 7.7g sugar, and only 3mg sodium (negligible for unsalted form). Potassium is exceptionally high at 1,025mg/100g. GI is approximately 15 (very low), with a glycaemic load of ~1.1 per 28g serving. Pistachios are the only common tree nut with meaningful lutein and zeaxanthin content (~1,200–1,600mcg/100g), supporting eye health. Dry roasting without salt preserves the natural flavour — slightly sweet, buttery, and mildly earthy — while maintaining the full nutritional profile. Clinical evidence from multiple RCTs (Kendall 2011, Parham 2014) supports pistachio consumption for T2DM glycaemic control at 25–85g/day. The pistachio paradox describes how in-shell eating reduces caloric intake by ~40–50% due to slower consumption pace. Pistachios belong to the Anacardiaceae family and may cross-react with cashew in tree-nut-allergic individuals. Widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Mustafa Centre. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12151. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC #12151, an approved primary source with exact match to dry-roasted, unsalted, shelled pistachio kernels. GI value of ~15 is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are verified exact matches. No estimation or analogy required.
notes: water_g set to -1: moisture content for dry-roasted pistachio kernels is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy FDC #12151 nutrient panel used for this record; consult USDA full nutrient report for moisture if required. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC #12151 — naturally zero in unprocessed tree nuts). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food contains no vitamin B12). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — pistachios are a plant food with no meaningful vitamin D content, confirmed per USDA FDC #12151). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food contains no dietary cholesterol). Monounsaturated fat (23.6g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (13.5g/100g) values are from USDA SR Legacy FDC #12151 fatty acid breakdown. The pistachio paradox: in-shell eating reduces caloric intake by approximately 40–50% due to the slower pace of consumption and visual cue of empty shells. Tree nut allergy caution: Pistacia vera belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and may cross-react with cashew nut allergy. GI source: University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283. GL per 28g serving calculated as (15 × 7.8g available carbohydrate per 28g serving) / 100 ≈ 1.2, consistent with input value of 1.1. Q8 fix (iteration 3): verified all aliases_by_language string values appear verbatim in flat aliases array; added 'Pista (sookha bhuna, bina namak)' to flat aliases to match romanized entry in aliases_by_language.
